display.purpose: Page titles and hero metric values. The 24px ceiling; the app never shouts.

body.purpose: Default text; prose capped 65-75ch, data may run denser.

label.purpose: Uppercase section titles, control labels, badges, sidebar group headers.

shadows.name: card-whisper
shadows.purpose: Resting shadow on KPI and stat-list cards. Reads as a surface, not a float.

shadows.name: sheet-overlay
shadows.purpose: The single real elevation: the right-anchored finding Sheet over the page it covers.

motion.name: control
motion.purpose: Button and nav-link hover / focus / border transitions.

motion.name: sheet-in
motion.purpose: Finding Sheet slide-in from the right (ease-out). Reduced-motion zeroes the duration.

breakpoints.name: phone
breakpoints.purpose: Finding Sheet goes full-bleed at or below this width.

breakpoints.name: content-max
breakpoints.purpose: Max width of the main block container.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one committing action per context (e.g. Run scan). Solid near-black, deliberately not blue.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background:#0a0a0a; color:#fafafa; border:1px solid transparent; border-radius:8px; font-weight:500; font-size:0.8125rem; padding:6px 14px; min-height:36px; box-shadow:0 1px 2px 0 rgba(0,0,0,0.04); cursor:pointer; transition:background .15s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background:#27272a; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #2563eb; outline-offset:2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: The everyday button: bordered surface with a one-pixel rest shadow.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background:rgba(0,0,0,0.025); color:#171717; border:1px solid #e6e6e9; border-radius:8px; font-weight:500; font-size:0.8125rem; padding:6px 14px; min-height:36px; box-shadow:0 1px 2px 0 rgba(0,0,0,0.04); cursor:pointer; transition:background .15s ease, border-color .15s ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background:rgba(0,0,0,0.05); border-color:rgba(0,0,0,0.14); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #2563eb; outline-offset:2px; }

components.name: Severity Badge
components.description: Severity pill: same-hue tint fill, darkened severity text token, leading CSS dot. Meaning carried by text and shape, not color alone.
components.html: Critical
components.css: .ds-sev-badge { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:4px; padding:2px 8px; border-radius:6px; font-size:0.75rem; font-weight:600; line-height:1.4; white-space:nowrap; font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums; } .ds-sev-badge--critical { background:color-mix(in srgb, #dc2626 14%, transparent); color:#b91c1c; } .ds-sev-dot { width:9px; height:9px; border-radius:50%; background:#dc2626; flex:none; }

components.name: KPI Card
components.description: Headline metric card: muted label with optional severity dot, large tabular value, good/bad delta chip (arrow shows direction, color shows good/bad).
components.html:
Critical
12
▲ +3
components.css: .ds-kpi-card { box-sizing:border-box; min-width:150px; padding:16px; border:1px solid #e6e6e9; border-radius:14px; background:rgba(0,0,0,0.025); box-shadow:0 1px 2px 0 rgba(0,0,0,0.05); font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,Inter,'Segoe UI',Roboto,sans-serif; } .ds-kpi-card__label { font-size:0.75rem; color:rgba(0,0,0,0.65); font-weight:500; display:flex; align-items:center; gap:5px; } .ds-kpi-dot { width:9px; height:9px; border-radius:50%; background:#dc2626; flex:none; } .ds-kpi-card__value { font-size:1.5rem; font-weight:600; color:#171717; letter-spacing:-0.02em; line-height:1.2; margin-top:6px; font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums; } .ds-kpi-card__delta { font-size:0.75rem; font-weight:500; margin-top:6px; font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums; } .ds-kpi-card__delta--bad { color:#b91c1c; }

components.name: Status Pill
components.description: SLA / state verdict: tinted background plus same-hue text. OK / Warn / Bad.
components.css: .ds-status-pill { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; padding:3px 9px; border-radius:6px; font-size:0.75rem; font-weight:500; } .ds-status-pill--bad { background:rgba(220,38,38,0.12); color:#b91c1c; }

components.name: SLA Bullet
components.description: Thin progress bar vs an SLA target, colored by the 90/70 policy thresholds (green >=90, amber >=70, red below).
components.css: .ds-sla-bullet { width:100%; height:8px; border-radius:4px; background:rgba(0,0,0,0.04); position:relative; overflow:hidden; } .ds-sla-bullet__fill { height:100%; border-radius:4px; background:#d97706; }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Link
components.description: shadcn-style sidebar item. Rounded hover tint; active item gets a neutral accent pill (not blue) and weight 600.
components.html: OS vulnerabilitiesMTTR & SLA
components.css: .ds-nav { display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:2px; background:#f8f8fa; padding:8px; border-radius:10px; width:220px; } .ds-nav-link { display:flex; align-items:center; gap:8px; border-radius:8px; padding:6px 8px; min-height:32px; color:rgba(0,0,0,0.65); font-size:0.8125rem; font-weight:450; text-decoration:none; transition:background .12s ease, color .12s ease; } .ds-nav-ico { width:16px; height:16px; border-radius:4px; background:rgba(0,0,0,0.25); flex:none; } .ds-nav-link:hover { background:rgba(0,0,0,0.05); color:#171717; } .ds-nav-link--active { background:rgba(0,0,0,0.08); color:#171717; font-weight:600; } .ds-nav-link:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #2563eb; outline-offset:2px; }

narrative.overview: This dashboard is a ledger of risk, not a billboard for it. Every figure on screen is meant to be defensible: a number a security analyst can act on and a leader can put in front of an auditor without flinching. The visual system earns trust the way a good ledger does, through exactness, consistency, and restraint. Surfaces are quiet near-white planes ruled by hairline borders; ink is near-black; figures are set in tabular numerals so columns line up and values never jitter as they update. Color is rationed like ink in a ledger and spent only on genuine meaning: a severity level, an SLA breach, a state change. Because the field is calm, a single critical-red badge reads instantly. The build emulates shadcn/ui entirely in CSS over native Streamlit theming, so it feels like a modern product tool rather than a security-vendor console. Components are tactile and confident: the primary action commits in solid near-black, controls sit at a comfortable 36px height, and surfaces carry a one-pixel shadow that makes them feel like real cards.
keyCharacteristics: Neutral by default; saturated color reserved for severity, state, and SLA verdicts.
Tabular numerals everywhere figures appear, so values stay aligned and stable.
Flat surfaces ruled by hairline borders; depth appears only on demand.
Meaning never carried by color alone: every severity and status pairs color with a dot, glyph, or label.
Familiar product-tool vocabulary (shadcn-grade), light theme only.

rules.name: The Rationed Ink Rule
rules.body: Saturated color is spent only on meaning: severity, state, or an SLA verdict. If a color is on screen, it is answering a question about risk. Neutral is the default; decorative color is forbidden.

rules.name: The Two-Token Severity Rule
rules.body: Severity always carries two tokens: a vivid fill for marks and dots, and a darker text token for any colored label. Never set severity text in the fill color on a tint; it will fail contrast.

rules.name: The Tabular Figures Rule
rules.body: Any number that updates or sits in a column is set in tabular numerals. Always. A jittering metric reads as untrustworthy.

rules.name: The No-Display-Font Rule
rules.body: Labels, buttons, and data are never set in a display or decorative face. One family, weight contrast only.

rules.name: The Whisper-Or-Lift Rule
rules.body: A surface gets either a one-pixel whisper (it is a card) or a real shadow (it is an overlay). Nothing in between. Mid-weight ambient shadows on in-page content are forbidden; that is the dated-app tell.

dos: Do keep the field neutral and ration saturated color to severity, state, and SLA verdicts.
Do pair every severity and status with a non-color signal: a dot, glyph, or text label.
Do use the two-token severity system: vivid fill for marks and dots, darker text token for any colored label.
Do set every figure in tabular numerals.
Do let the primary action commit in solid Graphite (#0a0a0a); keep Signal Blue (#2563eb) for data, focus, and accent.
Do define depth with hairline borders and the one-pixel card whisper; reserve real shadow for overlays like the Sheet.
Do keep edits to styles.css in sync with the native tokens in .streamlit/config.toml; native theming is the source of truth.
Do keep focus rings and a prefers-reduced-motion alternative on every interactive element and animation.
donts: Don't ship generic SaaS-cream with gradient accents or a big-number hero-metric template. The page is true white, the palette light-tuned.
Don't build noisy security-vendor theater: no walls of red and orange cells, no gauges, no blinking risk drama.
Don't fall into dated enterprise density: no gray-on-gray Splunk-style tables, no density without hierarchy.
Don't add consumer-playful flourish: no mascots, illustrations, or marketing decoration in a security tool.
Don't use a colored side-stripe (border-left greater than 1px) as a decorative accent on cards, list items, or alerts.
Don't apply background-clip: text gradient text anywhere. Emphasis comes from weight and size.
Don't introduce a second type family or a display font for labels, buttons, or data.
Don't add mid-weight ambient shadows to in-page content; a surface is either a one-pixel-whisper card or a real-shadow overlay.
